description: Fix visual asymmetry caused by retro offset shadows inside padded containers in the side panel. The theme uses flat offset shadows (right + down) that eat into right-side padding/margin, making the right gap visually smaller than the left gap.

userStories.title: Fix shadow-padding asymmetry in App.tsx main content area and search bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel plugin cards, empty states, and search input to have visually symmetric left/right spacing so the UI looks balanced.
acceptanceCriteria: The search bar wrapper (currently `px-4`) uses asymmetric padding: `pl-4 pr-5` to compensate for the Input's `shadow-md` (4px offset)
The main content area (currently `px-4`) uses asymmetric padding: `pl-4 pr-5` to compensate for Accordion.Item and Empty component `shadow-md` (4px offset)
Visual left gap equals visual right gap (measured from shadow edge to container edge) for all shadowed children
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx

Line 191: `<div className="px-4 pt-4 pb-2">` — contains `<Input>` which has built-in `shadow-md` (4px). Change to `pl-4 pr-5 pt-4 pb-2`.

Line 212: `<main className="flex-1 px-4 pb-2 ...">` — contains `<Accordion.Item>` cards with `shadow-md` (4px) and `<Empty>` states with `shadow-md` (4px). Change `px-4` to `pl-4 pr-5`.

The shadow definitions are in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css:
- `shadow-md` = `4px 4px 0 0 var(--border)` — 4px right, 4px down

Tailwind spacing: `pr-5` = 20px, `pl-4` = 16px, difference = 4px which exactly compensates for the 4px shadow offset. The visual gap from shadow right edge to container edge will equal the left gap (both 16px).

userStories.title: Fix shadow-margin asymmetry in ConfirmationDialog outer wrapper
userStories.description: As a user, I want the confirmation dialog card to have visually symmetric left/right margins so it looks centered in the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: The ConfirmationDialog outer wrapper (currently `mx-4`) uses asymmetric margin: `ml-4 mr-5` to compensate for the dialog card's `shadow-md` (4px offset)
Visual left margin equals visual right margin (measured from shadow edge to viewport edge)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx

Line 82: `<div className="mx-4 mt-2" role="alert">` — wraps a card with `shadow-md` (4px right offset). Change `mx-4` to `ml-4 mr-5`.

The card itself (line 83) has `shadow-md` = `4px 4px 0 0 var(--border)`. The shadow extends 4px to the right. With `ml-4 mr-5` (16px left, 20px right), the visual gap from shadow right edge to viewport = 20 - 4 = 16px, matching the left gap.

userStories.title: Fix shadow-padding asymmetry in ConfirmationDialog actions bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want the confirmation dialog action buttons to have visually symmetric left/right spacing within the actions row.
acceptanceCriteria: The actions bar (currently `px-3`) uses asymmetric padding: `pl-3 pr-3.5` to compensate for Button size='sm' `shadow` (3px offset)
Visual left gap equals visual right gap (measured from rightmost button's shadow edge to container edge)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx

Line 128: `<div className="border-accent-foreground flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 border-t-2 px-3 py-2">` — contains `<Button size="sm">` components. The `sm` size applies `shadow` = `3px 3px 0 0 var(--border)` (3px right offset).

Change `px-3` to `pl-3 pr-3.5`. Tailwind: `pr-3.5` = 14px, `pl-3` = 12px, difference = 2px. Wait — the shadow is 3px. There's no exact Tailwind class for 15px. Use `pr-[15px]` for exact compensation, or `pr-3.5` (14px) which is close (1px off). Actually, since `--shadow` is `3px 3px 0 0`, we need 3px extra. `pl-3` = 12px, we need right = 15px. Use arbitrary value `pr-[15px]`.

Actually, looking at the CLAUDE.md theme rules: 'No arbitrary values (`bg-[#ffdb33]`, `rounded-[8px]`)'. So use `pr-4` (16px) instead — it slightly overcompensates by 1px but uses standard Tailwind classes. The visual difference between 12px left and 13px effective right (16-3) is negligible and far better than the current 9px effective right.

Change `px-3` to `pl-3 pr-4`.

userStories.title: Fix shadow-padding asymmetry in Footer
userStories.description: As a user, I want the footer to have visually symmetric left/right spacing accounting for the shadows on the icon buttons and NumberStepper.
acceptanceCriteria: The footer (currently `px-3`) uses asymmetric padding: `pl-3 pr-3.5` to compensate for the NumberStepper's `shadow-sm` (2px offset) on the right edge
Visual left gap equals visual right gap (measured from NumberStepper shadow edge to container edge)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx

Line 51: `<footer className="border-border bg-card sticky bottom-0 flex items-center justify-between border-t-2 px-3 py-3 text-sm">` — the right side contains `<PortEditor>` which renders `<NumberStepper>` with `shadow-sm` = `2px 2px 0 0 var(--border)` (2px right offset).

The left side has `<Button size="icon" variant="outline">` with `shadow-md` (4px). However, the left buttons' shadow extends rightward into the gap between elements, not into the left container padding. So the left padding is visually correct.

The right-side NumberStepper shadow extends 2px past its border toward the container edge. Change `px-3` to `pl-3 pr-3.5`. Tailwind: `pr-3.5` = 14px, `pl-3` = 12px, difference = 2px which exactly compensates for the 2px shadow offset.
